calendar.matters:
Petition For An Order To Confirm Successor Trustee And For Instructions Re Distribution Of Trust Assets
Off Calendar Nov-03-2020 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dewey Harpainter, Esq., counsel for Karen Field, Administrator, Grace Chou Estate (appearing by video conference, audio only)

Mr. Harpainter advises the Court that the parties have agreed to dismiss the proceedings pending in the San Francisco Superior Court and pursue the same in a more suitable venue. Mr. Harpainter reports that he has filed the dismissal of his client's Petition - which the Court confirms - and further represents that the dismissal of Ms. Hill's Petition is pending.
In the absence of Mr. Hall and his client Ms. Hill, and in the absence of the filed dismissal for that Petition, the Court denies the Hill Petition.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Deputy: K. Kabasares; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

Petition By Administrator With Will Annexed To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Real Property And For Order Directing Its Transfer To Estate; Removal Of Trustee, And Accounting With Exhibits 1-7 [Probate Code 850, 17200]
Off Calendar Nov-03-2020 Off Calendar

Petition For An Order To Confirm Successor Trustee And For Instructions Re Distribution Of Trust Assets
Off Calendar Oct-06-2020 Continued To Nov-03-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition By Administrator With Will Annexed To Establish Estates Claim Of Ownership To Real Property And For Order Directing Its Transfer To Estate; Removal Of Trustee, And Accounting With Exhibits 1-7 [Probate Code 850, 17200]
Off Calendar Oct-06-2020 Continued To Nov-03-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dawn Ward, Esq., counsel for and with Tina Hill, Administrator (will annexed) (appearing separately by video conference); Dewey Harpainter, Esq. and Elizabeth Ikemire, Esq. counsel for and with Karen Field, Petitioner (appearing separately by video conference).

At the outset the Court queries counsel as to proper venue for the Petition, noting that the proper venue pursuant to Probate Code Sec. 17005(a)(1) is where the trust is administered. Since the trustee resides in Kings County, California, venue would ostensibly be proper there. Situs of the real property would only give rise to venue in the absence of a trustee. Mr. Harpainter responds, citing Probate Code Sec. 850 which upon review, the Court rejects as inapposite. Mr. Harpainter further advises that the matter is set for arbitration in San Jose on February 5, 2021 and from the perspective of judicial economy, it may be best to hold the current motions in abeyance pending the outcome of mediation. Ms. Ward confirms that the trustee is resident in Kings County.

Ms. Ward advises the Court that her client's separate Petition was continued to November 3, 2020 due to a logistics issue wherein courtesy copies were not timely received by the Probate Examiner. Ms. Ikemire advises the Court as to the plethora of related cases pending in a variety of counties, arguing for retention of the trust matters in San Francisco.

Noting the continuance of Ms. Hill's petition, the Court continues hearing on the instant matter to November 3, 2020 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court will entertain final briefing on the issue of venue which briefs must be filed on or before October 27, 2020 with courtesy copies and proposed Orders to the Examiner on or before that date as well.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported; Bailiff: Deputy Johnson. (D204)
